facts: A magnet attracts some metals like iron and steel, but it does not attract wood, plastic, or paper because those materials are not magnetic.
When the north pole of one magnet faces the north pole of another magnet, they push each other away — that pushing away is called repulsion.
A magnet can pull an iron paperclip toward it even without touching it, because the magnetic force works through the air.
misconceptions: Children often think a magnet sticks to any shiny metal, like aluminum foil or copper pennies, because all metals look similar.
